task: D4.4 launch_bounds retune after fusion changes
summary: No per-kernel __launch_bounds__ retune was applied. The D1.1-D1.6 fusion sweep did not retain a default rotation/RMSNorm/projection kernel that changes the default resource envelope; D4.2 also rejected the batched-fusion plan. Static launch-bound guards show current wrappers do not bypass source max-thread bounds. Existing parent/hipEngine probes already rejected the plausible stricter-bound/thread-count levers.

resource_metadata.command: HIPENGINE_PARO_ROTATE_DUAL_PACK8_FUSED=1 rocprofv3 --kernel-trace --output-format csv --output-directory /tmp/hipengine-d11/rocprof --output-file d11_rotate_fused -- python3 scripts/qwen35_paro_bench.py --max-layers 1 --prompt-length 4 --decode-tokens 2 --warmup-decode-tokens 0 --token-id 9707 --graph-replay-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --json /tmp/hipengine-d11/rocprof-bench.json

D1.1_rotate_staged.decision: The only rotate-once single-kernel design that preserves pack8 layout needs global scratch plus a device-side barrier. Under one-step graph replay, the removed rotate launch is cheaper than the barrier/spin/global-staging cost; full-attention Q/K also does not reduce launch count because V still needs a separate rotate. Recomputing rotation per output pack remains disallowed by the parent D4 rejection.

D1.2_D1.3_D1.6_and_D4.2.D1.2: deferred/no-op; no material single-use RMSNorm/add-RMSNorm producer
D1.2_D1.3_D1.6_and_D4.2.D1.3: rejected/no-op; no remaining same-input projection fusion with arithmetic/data-reuse upside
D1.2_D1.3_D1.6_and_D4.2.D1.6: rejected as default; K/V route changes pairing, not launch count

marlin_kernel_rows.kernel_name: void (anonymous namespace)::gemv_paro_marlin_k_fma_kernel<_Float16>(_Float16 const*, int const*, int const*, _Float16 const*, _Float16*, long, long, int)

D2.1_marlin_k_retained_w4_decode.thread_choice_function: marlin_k_default_threads(in_features, out_features) returns 128 for Qwen3.5 decode shapes and never exceeds the __launch_bounds__(128, 4) max.

interpretation: The current lm-head W8A16 launch already uses the fastest probed workgroup size: 128 threads. 64 is ~2.1% slower on the isolated kernel; 256 and 512 are much worse from reduction/wave overhead.
The current legacy shared-expert lowp W8A16 launches already use the fastest probed workgroup size: 64 threads. Larger workgroups regress both gate/up and down shapes.

The existing fused shared gate/up+SiLU helper was probed as a decode-shape tile/fusion alternative; its best isolated median (~5.04 us at 512 threads) is slower than generic lowp gate/up at 64 threads plus the measured graph shared SiLU (~3.22 us + ~1.28 us), so it is not a c=1 decode replacement.

Resource metadata shows no scratch, no SGPR/VGPR spills, low VGPR pressure, wave32, and tiny dynamic LDS reductions; the remaining lm-head cost is dominated by the unavoidable ~509 MB int8 weight stream per token, while the small shared linears are already launch/reduction efficient.

prior_negative_launch_bounds_worklog.decision: rejected/reverted; stricter bound caused spills and large bucket regressions, keep __launch_bounds__(32, 8)

rationale: The D1 fusion work that would have changed the default rotation/RMSNorm/W4 fused-kernel resource envelope was rejected, deferred, or launch-count neutral; D4.2 closed the stacked-D1 plan as unsupported.
Current W4/WMMA source kernels with __launch_bounds__ are launched at or below their max-thread bound: pack8/selected/Marlin-K <=128, compact WMMA/fusedW4 prefill ==32.

Existing thread-count/resource probes do not identify a safe retune: rotate-staged and Marlin-K show VGPR=104/scratch=0/LDS=512 at workgroup 128, but rotate-staged regresses and Marlin-K is already retained; W8A16 larger workgroups regress; prior stricter WMMA/W4 launch_bounds trials regressed or spilled.
Per project boundary, new micro-tuning loops belong in ~/amd-gpu-tuning/ and should be ported only after parent evidence identifies a stable launch_bounds change.
decision.future_reopen_condition: Reopen only after a default rotation/RMSNorm/W4 fusion is retained or parent kernel R&D produces a source-level launch_bounds retune with resource metadata and throughput evidence; first rerun the static no-bypass audit and a kernel-trace smoke.

validation.result: passed; artifact generator asserted source launch_bounds inventory, wrapper thread guards, and no accepted wrapper thread count above the source launch bound
